# References {.unnumbered}

## Further reading

- **Happy Git and GitHub for the useR** [@bryan-happygit], <https://happygitwithr.com>. The standard practical reference for using Git and GitHub from R and RStudio. The learner pre-work includes its "Why Git? Why GitHub?" introduction (@sec-learner-setup).
- **Openscapes illustrations** [@openscapes-illustrations] by Allison Horst and Julia Stewart Lowndes, <https://github.com/openscapes>. Friendly, collaboration-first illustrations used in the workshop slides; they set the tone described in @sec-philosophy.
- **Teaching with Posit Cloud** [@posit-cloud-teach], <https://docs.posit.co/cloud/guide/teach/>. The setup guide for the cloud fallback environment noted in @sec-learner-setup.

## Tools and services

- R, <https://cran.r-project.org>
- RStudio, <https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/>
- Git, <https://git-scm.com>
- GitHub, <https://github.com>
- Quarto, <https://quarto.org>
- Element / Matrix, <https://element.io>
- Posit Cloud, <https://posit.cloud>
- Plausible analytics, <https://plausible.io>
- Zenodo, <https://zenodo.org>

::: {#refs}
:::
